creator: markj@FreeBSD.org
text: Whether the lock is held doesn't really matter.  At this point the driver owns the mbuf.  The lock only protects driver state, so if, say, it updates a descriptor to point to the mbuf buffer, we'd want to ensure that the pointer is cleared before the mbuf is freed or handed off to other layers.  But the actual m_freem() call does not require any synchronization.
